aliases: Indonesian stuffed sweet pancake with cheese and condensed milk

english: Indonesian stuffed sweet pancake with cheese and condensed milk

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for martabak manis (Indonesian stuffed sweet pancake). FOODON_03301710 (pancake food product) is used as the closest parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (Pancakes) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for martabak manis with cheese and condensed milk filling.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for martabak manis. SNOMED CT does not have a specific concept for this Indonesian stuffed sweet pancake.

preparation_method: filled with cheese and condensed milk

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is the full 24cm martabak manis divided into 10 pieces, weighing approximately 500g total, typically shared between 2–4 people.

nutrient_source.title: Pancake, plain, prepared from recipe — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 172764), adapted with cheese and condensed milk filling components from USDA FoodData Central entries for cheddar cheese (FDC ID 173414) and sweetened condensed milk (FDC ID 175215)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Martabak manis is not present in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted composition: approximately 55% pancake batter base (USDA SR Legacy FDC 172764), 25% cheddar cheese (USDA SR Legacy FDC 173414), and 20% sweetened condensed milk (USDA SR Legacy FDC 175215), reflecting the described recipe of buttery pancake stuffed with premium cheddar cheese and drizzled with condensed milk. Per-100g values are derived from this weighted blend and rounded to one decimal place. Confidence is medium due to estimation from components.

gi_source.title: Pancake, made from mix — Glycemic Index Database, University of Sydney (Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for martabak manis. GI of 67 is adopted from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for plain pancake made from mix (GI ~67, medium category), which is the closest analogous food. Martabak manis has a similar refined-flour batter base; the addition of cheese and condensed milk increases fat and sugar content but does not substantially alter the GI of the carbohydrate fraction. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (67 × 192.5g carbohydrate in 500g serving) / 100 = 129.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~290mg/100g) and high saturated fat from cheese and butter
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 1–2 pieces (50–100g) due to moderate sodium content (~145–290mg per portion) and high saturated fat from cheddar cheese and butter, which may contribute to vascular stiffness; avoid pairing with other high-sodium foods in the same meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (GL ~129.0 per full serving) and high sugar content from condensed milk (~16.2g sugar/100g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit martabak manis cheesy milk due to its very high glycemic load per full serving (~129.0 GL), high refined carbohydrate content (~38.5g/100g), and significant added sugar from condensed milk (~16.2g/100g), all of which can cause rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation; if consumed, restrict to 1 piece (~50g) and pair with a protein-rich food to blunt the glycemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (~8.1g/100g) and cholesterol (~72mg/100g) from cheddar cheese, butter, and eggs
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat martabak manis cheesy milk as an occasional indulgence only, as the high saturated fat content (~8.1g/100g) from cheddar cheese and butter, combined with dietary cholesterol (~72mg/100g) from eggs and dairy, may adversely affect LDL cholesterol levels and increase cardiovascular risk when consumed regularly or in large portions.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Martabak Manis Cheesy Milk (10 pieces) is an Indonesian stuffed sweet pancake (martabak manis), a beloved street food originating from Indonesia and widely popular across Southeast Asia, including Singapore where it is sold by specialty vendors such as Smile Martabak. The dish consists of a thick, fluffy, yeast-leavened wheat-flour batter cooked in a round cast-iron mould approximately 24cm in diameter, producing a spongy, golden-brown pancake that is generously filled with premium cheddar cheese and drizzled with sweetened condensed milk before being folded and cut into 10 pieces. The texture is soft and pillowy on the inside with a slightly crisp buttery exterior; the flavour profile is rich, sweet, and savoury simultaneously, with the sharpness of melted cheddar balanced by the sweetness of condensed milk. Per 100g, the dish provides approximately 310 kcal, 9.2g protein, 13.8g fat (8.1g saturated), 38.5g carbohydrate, and 16.2g sugar, making it a calorie-dense, high-carbohydrate, high-fat food. The full 500g serving carries an estimated glycemic load of approximately 129.0, placing it in the very high glycemic load category. Clinically, this food is of high concern for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its high refined carbohydrate and sugar content, and of high concern for those with cardiovascular disease due to its elevated saturated fat and cholesterol from cheese, butter, and eggs. Sodium content (~290mg/100g) warrants moderate caution for individuals with hypertension. Martabak manis is also known as terang bulan in some Indonesian regions and is typically consumed as a dessert or late-night snack, often shared among a group. It is not suitable for vegans or those with gluten or dairy allergies.

confidence_reason: Martabak manis cheesy milk is not present in any primary regional approved source (HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted blend of USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for plain pancake, cheddar cheese, and sweetened condensed milk, which are approved secondary sources. GI is estimated from the closest analogous food (plain pancake from mix) in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Food group corrected from 'Beverages' (as in input) to 'Desserts': martabak manis is a stuffed sweet pancake, not a beverage; the input food_group appears to be a data entry error from the field survey platform categorisation. 2. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composition model (55% pancake batter, 25% cheddar cheese, 20% condensed milk) using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy approved sources; no direct laboratory analysis or primary regional database entry exists for this specific product. 3. GI of 67 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database (plain pancake from mix); no direct GI measurement for martabak manis was found in approved sources. 4. monounsaturated_fat_g (3.9), polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.8), trans_fat_g (0.2), potassium_mg (185.0), calcium_mg (195.0), iron_mg (1.1), cholesterol_mg (72.0), vitamin_c_mg (0.5), vitamin_a_mcg_rae (98.0), vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg (0.12), vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg (0.22), vitamin_b3_niacin_mg (0.9), vitamin_b6_mg (0.06), vitamin_b12_mcg (0.5), folate_mcg_dfe (28.0), vitamin_d_mcg (0.4), vitamin_e_mg (0.6), phosphorus_mg (210.0), magnesium_mg (18.0), zinc_mg (1.1), selenium_mcg (12.0), and water_g (36.5) are all estimated from the weighted USDA component blend; none are -1 sentinel values as all were derivable from the approved source blend. 5. Halal status is assumed based on the described ingredients (no pork or alcohol); actual halal certification of the specific restaurant product was not verified. 6. The cuisine_tag 'Coffee' in the input appears to be a platform artefact and is not reflected in this record.
